beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, check team messages and stream setup for the week.
beats.reasoning: Maren wakes early on a Monday with full energy, typical habit-creature routine. Coffee and work prep are part of the morning beat. Sunny day ahead suits her mood.
Work day starts early; Light Rail closure (9am–3pm Westlake–UW) doesn't affect her Interbay route. She drives or takes a bus that avoids that corridor.

beats.activity: Coach practice session with competitive team, review replays and strategy.
beats.reasoning: Standard Monday morning coaching block. She's at the facility before the 11am FIFA match, focused on team prep.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales FIFA World Cup match at a downtown sports bar.
beats.reasoning: The 11am kick-off is perfect timing. Maren is a thrill-seeker and creature of habit—she'd catch this match live at a bar with close friends or fellow sports enthusiasts, not alone at home. Downtown bars open early for the match.

beats.activity: Lunch at Pike Place Market, grab fresh fish and produce.
beats.reasoning: Post-match, she's already downtown and sunny weather invites outdoor exploration. She picks up ingredients for home brewing or evening dinner—fits her hobby and values local, fresh goods.
Returning to afternoon coaching sessions. She's energized from the match and social time.

beats.activity: Afternoon coaching: scrim with rival team, one-on-ones with players, film review.
beats.reasoning: Typical Monday afternoon workload. Competitive coaching requires long, focused blocks. Light Rail closure doesn't affect her facility.

beats.activity: Commute home, stop at local fly-fishing tackle shop on the way.
beats.reasoning: Sunny early June is perfect fly-fishing season. Maren carries a film camera and values thrill-seeking activities; she grabs new flies or line on the way home, feeds her hobby.
Return to Capitol Hill home, settling in for evening.

beats.activity: Home brewing: prep ingredients and start a batch, take photos with film camera.
beats.reasoning: Maren is a home brewer and carries a film camera everywhere. Perfect evening activity on a sunny day—she enjoys the creative process and documents it photographically.

beats.activity: Light dinner, review tomorrow's coaching schedule, wind down with a book.
beats.reasoning: She's already home, winding down for the evening. Creature of habit, she reviews work for Monday and relaxes before bed.
Full 8-hour sleep cycle. She had a full, energetic day and is ready to rest. Wakes refreshed for Tuesday morning.

diary: Started the morning with strong coffee and a quick scan of the team's Discord—everyone's prepped for the week, which is always a relief. The commute to Interbay was smooth; I caught the tail end of rush hour. Practice went well, the players were sharp during replays, though I noticed one of our mid-laners is still overextending. Couldn't resist catching the USA-Wales match at Belltown Sports Bar around 11—grabbed a beer and watched it unfold with other fans. The energy was fun, even if the result wasn't what we hoped for. Pike Place was packed as always, but I scored fresh salmon and some good greens. The afternoon scrim with the rival team was intense; we took some valuable losses that'll help us iterate. Stopped by the fly shop in Ballard on the way home—been meaning to grab new tippet material. Spent the evening prepping a new homebrew batch, took some photos with the film camera. There's something meditative about both coaching and brewing; both require patience and precision.
thought_process: The Light Rail closure didn't affect me much since I drive to Interbay anyway, but I was glad to avoid the downtown transit mess. The FIFA match was a nice mental break mid-day—I don't follow soccer closely, but the communal atmosphere at the bar recharged me before the afternoon grind. I deliberately built in that fly-fishing errand because I've been neglecting my hobbies lately; coaching is consuming, and I needed to remind myself that life exists outside the esports bubble. The brewing session felt like the right way to end the day—creative, tactile, something completely separate from yelling callouts and analyzing frame data. My mood shifted from task-focused this morning to more reflective by evening, which felt earned.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
